content:
Good morning, folks. Today is June 13, 2023, and CPI numbers are due out this morning. It is important
that you try not to get ahead of that because it can be somewhat destructive if you're incorrect in your
assumptions or your analysis. I stand on the sidelines of this report. I've been burned by it many times.
Today's lecture will include a quick pass through the ES (E-mini S&P), Euro Dollar, Cable, the Dollar Index,
NASDAQ, Dow futures, and a peek at Gold. I want to talk a little bit about where setups form and how you
can trust the principles I'm teaching, but we're going to watch CPI live.

section_title: LABORATORY EXPERIMENT APPROACH TO CPI TRADING
content:
It's important you also understand that anything I tell you today should be viewed from the safety of a
laboratory experiment. That means you're not even trying to trade a demo account. You're not even
tempted at all to take any trade based on what I'm going to tell you today.
I will not come back later and say, 'you want to do so and so, here's what I did,' because I'm going to be
here right here live with you.

- WEEKLY AND DAILY
Let's start with the Dollar Index, specifically the weekly chart. We are at the bottom of an Inversion Gap
here. Originally, we treated that as an Inversion Gap that would come down and act as support to take us
up into our premium arrays. We have since worked lower back inside this Gap.
I mentioned in the last commentary that I want to see if it wants to go outside of this and then treat this
shaded area here as resistance. So far, we've come down and respected the low of it. Dropping into a daily
chart, we have somewhat of a small remaining Gap here with an Order Block. I'll be watching that going
into the rest of this week.

section_title: CPI TRADING CAUTION AND POST-EVENT STRATEGY
content:
This does not mean for you to go in here and assume that the dollar is going to sell off right away because
I see these buttons there. I want to see if CPI supports this idea. That's why I tell you, don't stand ahead or
get in front of the CPI number, because it can be very one-sided. It could smoke you easily. Your stop loss
will not protect you; it won't have any protective characteristics at all because price can Gap right on
through and the liquidity is pulled.
Many people want to trade it, but brokers and liquidity providers pull the liquidity; they don't let you connect.
Because of that, you want to stand aside, let the initial chaos hit the marketplace, let the victims start
bleeding, and then once the impact crater has been created, then you go around and look for the easy
targets short-term liquidity runs or runs to inefficiencies.
We treat that surge in price action by the CPI number as a means of creating that first initial shock. Imagine
the surface of a pond, real smooth, mirror-like, and then CPI is going to be like a boulder being dropped in
the middle of it. That first splash
- we don't want to be harmed by that, and I don't want any of you, my
students, being harmed by that. But once that occurs, there are lots of new ripples, and every advancing
outward-moving circle from that impact are all opportunities. Each one of them are respective to their
specific time frame, so there are setups that are forming all the time across all different one-minute, second
charts, 15-minute charts; there's all kinds of opportunities, and whatever one suits you is the one that's for
you.
For the DXY, I want to see if it wants to get below this, then treat this as resistance and start to gravitate
towards this. I'm not suggesting it will do it in one fell swoop today, but obviously it can, because this type of
market driver can create those types of runs. But I want to see does it want to get down into here and then
reprice to this inefficiency between these candles' high and this candle's low; more specifically, the opening
price of this down-close candle on the daily chart.

- ORDER BLOCK AND INEFFICIENCY
Let's go over to Euro. In similar fashion, we have a very short Order Block here, a very small little Gap. I'm
going to show you the tooth level so you can see there's a separation; it's not much, but it's there. So this
Order Block involves reaching up into this down-closed candle's range, repricing to the separation between
these two candles. So this candle beneath my cursor, that candle's low, and this candle's high, the candle
right above my cursor, that candle's high. That small little inefficiency I want to see does CPI lend well to
it wanting to get up into that area? It's a small little pool of liquidity in the form of buy stops that will be
resting about this. You don't need any kind of market map, like Bookmap, to see this. If you study price
action naked, you'll see above swing highs and short-term highs and lows, there's obvious stops in liquidity.

section_title: EURUSD BEARISH SCENARIO AND BROADER MARKET CONTEXT
content:
When the market has moved away from this Gap here, traded above the short-term high here, relative
equal highs, I want to see does it have a willingness to use the CPI number to expand into this direction?
Now, if we take out this low here and very specific, get below the consequent encroachment of this Wick,
why this one? Because the difference between the candles' open and close on this one inside of the Gap,
this one over here, it left that Fair Value Gap; there was a change in the state of delivery right there.
So if we get through the consequent encroachment of this Wick, that to me spells bearishness on Euro
Dollar, and the dollar might surprise to the upside. But as long as that does not happen, I'm looking for this
Order Block and this Gap. For the slow folks, I did not give you a two-scenario; I'm telling you what I'm
looking for because I don't know at the time, at the very time we're sitting here live, I don't know. If I knew,
I'd be trading it. It's CPI, I have no idea what they're going to do. CPI is one of those events like FOMC rate
announcements, which we'll have tomorrow.

section_title: CPI/FOMC WEEK STRATEGY AND MARKET INTERVENTION
content:
This week is littered with landmines. I teach my students if you have a week like this, you want to try to get
yourself on Monday. Monday was a pretty good day. I'm looking for evidence to support a bias for the rest of
the week, so it'll give me insight to see how they use this data.
Manual intervention is where the quote-unquote 'powers that be,' you call them market makers, they are
the folks in charge of how far these markets will go, when they'll move, and what price is going to be,
period. It has nothing to do with your buying and selling pressure, all of our gimmicks, all of our Order
Blocks, all of our supply and demand, harmonic patterns, none of that matters when they step in. They will
literally reprice to another price level, and it doesn't matter what carnage is created from that. So that's the
reason why I'm telling you that this type of market driver, CPI, is dangerous. You can't trust even my own
concepts in front of a move like this because it can roll right over top of it.
I have been very candid about how I get many times this very CPI number wrong. It's only very few times
I've been able to publicly call it and be correct. So I want you to respect the measure of risk. Do not look at
anything I'm saying today as a little nudge to say 'here, here's our secret little handshake, go take this
trade.' Please don't do that. If you're right, it was luck, I promise you. On a day like today with CPI, if you
make money, it's luck. That's the only time luck exists when it's a complete uncertainty and you just happen
to be on the side that worked. I don't trade with luck, I don't rely on luck. It doesn't exist. You make the
results, and skill set and experience dictate that.
So that's what I'm looking for. I favor this right now so that way you understand what's going on. But if I'm
wrong, where do I concede and say I'm wrong, and then I look at the market from the opposite end? As I
mentioned, it needs to get below the consequent encroachment of this Wick because that's where the state
of delivery changed. Inside this Gap, instead of seeing us run higher, we've taken a pool of liquidity out
here, and we went a little bit higher, but these are relative equal highs. I don't know if they're using that right
there, and then CPI sinks us, and they make aggressive runs for sell side below here. I don't know. So it's
important for you to understand that there are times where your analysis, your experience, your model will
be silent. It does not mean it's broken, it doesn't mean that they changed the algorithm; it just means that
you have to sit still and you have to exercise discipline and self-control and wait for the market to give you
your setup. You're not trying to be like a crack fiend out there with your crack pipe every single day trying to
smoke pips and points in these markets because it's moving around. You just got to sit still. So that's the
two things I'm looking for for Fiber (EURUSD).

I'll take a quick peek at Cable. All right, Cable, I believe this is nice. Came down inside this Buy Side
Imbalance-Fair Value Gap, which is a Fair Value Gap, but it's in a Buy Side Imbalance also an efficiency. It
trades down to it here and then look at the reaction so far today. So I want to see us return back up into this
Gap. I'll show you in a second where that's anchored, it's on a weekly chart. That's that inefficiency right
there. So I want to see does it return back into that? If it does, it has unfinished business all in here. That's
assuming, obviously, if the dollar falls off.
Let's go back into a daily. So far, Cable's been just hanging around this large consolidation, not a whole lot
of activity. Even though we came down into this Gap and traded into it and showed initial willingness to go
higher, we have to overcome this. So it's going to have to make a real meaningful move outside of that
range here. The real level I'm trying to be interested in is here. So if we trade above it, fall short and come
back down to the middle of this inefficiency, that would be consequent encroachment in the form of
potential support. So if we run up, fail to get to here, that shaded area, like run above and come back down,
the lower time frames might give us an idea after CPI that would be something I would look for. It's not a
trade idea, it's just something I'm running certain terms of scenarios: what do I look for, what would I wait
for after CPI? If you don't trade ahead of CPI, what are you looking for after CPI? What are those types of
things? It's these things here I'm trying to outline to you.

section_title: NASDAQ (NQ) ANALYSIS AND FUTURES CONTRACT INFORMATION
content:
Let's take a quick look at NQ. We are looking at the September delivery contract month now. It's symbol 'U',
so NQU2023. I saw a guy post a tweet to me saying he was looking for NQQ. 'Q' is the month code for
August, and August is not a delivery month for index futures. It's only March (H), June (M), September (U),
and Z for December. There's only four contract delivery months for indexes or indices, I should say.
All right, so here is the weekly view on NQ. Like I said, this was the performer on the upside, the strongest
one, and it has delivered. And drop down to a daily. This one here, only experience speaking here, because
we have a rate announcement (FOMC) really afternoon tomorrow (Wednesday) and we have the CPI
number this morning, which will be out in about four minutes or so, a little less than four minutes, I gotta
hurry up. This sell side looks suspect, even though we had a really nice run up in here. I just feel like they're
going to make an attempt to get into this this week. Will it be today or tomorrow? I don't know. But this looks
too clean, way, way, way too clean. And even if it went down there, that would upset folks thinking that it's
topped. And then to me, if it went down below that and rallied and created another short-term inefficiency,
that would be bullish that could continue going higher. This would upset those individuals that see this as
support or a real good area to trail stop losses below that. I'm not trying to pick the top. I see CPI, I look at
that number that comes out as a means of trying to smash anyone that's profitable. It can, in some
instances, be used to continue or capitulate a move. That means something that's already in a movement
like this, it could accelerate and add the last piece of it. So again, that's the reason why I'm not getting
ahead of it and trying to trade it.

And then the Dow. This one here I favor a run above this high here. I think that would be something to
expect because it's been the 'sixth sister' of all three indices. NASDAQ, I told you, would be the leader. Our
focus has been teaching through the price delivery of E-mini S&P. And the Dow, I use it as a barometer, like
I use the Dollar Index. I don't trade the Dollar Index, but I use it as a risk on/risk off for comparative studies
and relative strength studies. For the indices, I use the Dow, so for like Dow Theory, SMT divergences,
sixth sister concepts, those types of things, I lean on the Dow for that purpose. We have a little bit of an
inefficiency right in here, so we could if we're going to drop initially on CPI, it could go down there and then
make a run up into that. That would be a scenario I would watch and see if it manifests today, and it's not a
trade idea, so please don't take that as such. Quickly go back into ES. We'll drop into a lower time frame.
Gold. Um, I don't know, there you go, I've gone to my head. I would say it's going to run for the sell side
below here. There you go, that's what I think. I'm probably wrong, please don't trade it. I wouldn't touch this
at all.

section_title: E-MINI S&P (ES) AND CPI LIVE SETUP
content:
ES, let's drop down to a 15-minute time frame. Smaller Gap in here. Buy side rests above here. So 30
seconds or so, everybody that's, you know, trading is going to regret having been in it. And everybody that
gets lucky, you're posted on social media and offer signals for purchase. So here's where our sell side is.
I'm going to try to keep the annotations like this, focus on my viewpoints. Sell side's here, buy side here,
inefficiency. It's great if I can get down here before it pops off.

- ES AND NASDAQ REACTION
So it ran up and hit our Fair Value Gap I was talking about, ran through it a little bit, that's okay. What's this
mean? Threshold of here, then swept the sell side, now buy side's next. Just a quick look at NASDAQ. So
it's taken its sell side here then ran for buy side.

- CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT OBJECTIVE
Quick look at the daily on the Dow. Yeah, I like this high. So if I have a wick like this, I'll measure the
midpoint, which is consequent encroachment. That would be first objective, and then I want to see if it
wants to get above here. Because I could be wrong, because it may use this initial pump to feed into that,
and tomorrow's rate announcement do something completely opposed to what I've outlined and not even
come up here, but I think that this is a likely draw on down.

- A TRAP FOR RETAIL TRADERS
So we've had buy side here and sell side taken. The initial draw, let's go into a one-minute chart. In one
moment, in one minute, the market opened here, ran up just a little bit above the Fair Value Gap, then
slammed down to take sell side, and then in the same minute, one candle closed up here after running up
the buy side. So in one minute, look how much the market has traversed.
Now, do you believe that you're going to be nimble enough to trade in that when the liquidity is already
pulled anyway? Your broker's not going to let you trade that. You're not going to see these guys out there
that put a buy order and a sell order, unless they're running their white list fake broker, they're running on
their own scam. Those orders will get filled because it's not really happening. But in the real market, none
of that works. You're not going to be allowed to be trading it; it will not let you fill.
As much as I love having fun and ripping other people, and I can take a good ribbing too, it's important that
when it matters, when real discussions about risk and money and the potential loss of it, I don't sugarcoat
any of that. So I'm telling you, if anybody's out there showing you examples where they get filled and they
got a really good entry here and they got out here, that's total grade A fake. They didn't get filled, they didn't
trade it. And anybody tells you otherwise, demand they do that and show you broker statements, log into
their broker's account and show you, not MT4 or MT5 account because that's too easily manipulated, but a
reputable broker. Sorry, but that that's just the facts. I consider myself a little bit better than the average
bear, and I don't believe I can make my stuff work even in these environments because it's highly
manipulated. You are absolutely up against a brick wall, and you won't be permitted, period, because it
moves so fast. Brokers can take, because they A-Book a lot of people, they could be wrong by
happenstance, and overleveraging Larry that can be in there doing big, big moves and get lucky. They have
to, they have to pay that out because of the fierceness and velocity of how fast it moves around. You know,
that's just that's how the business works, folks. I mean, you can be mad about it or just understand that's
what takes place and respect it. It's okay. I'm not going to play in that sandbox, I'll wait.

- ALGORITHM PRECISION
So, this is literally a 50/50 now because it's taken both sides of the liquidity after I drew your attention to
working off the Fair Value Gap I mentioned first. The takeaway in this is I want you to think about how we
were looking at the buy side and the sell side. Forget being wrong, forget profitability. Think, okay, think
about this: all of the movement that took place thus far was all inside of a one-minute basis or two minutes,
that's it. So all of this movement from here and here, that happened in a blink of an eye.
You would not have the ability, if you didn't have limit orders in that could be filled (not that they would be),
you wouldn't have the ability to adjust or to comprehend what it is that you should be doing next, especially
if you're a new student or a new trader. You have no idea what you're doing. You have no idea where you
would measure a willingness to see continuation up or down. Maybe you didn't even know where these
pools of liquidity were before I mentioned them. So if you don't know those things, why would you even
gamble on this report that's coming out or that came out six minutes or so ago? It's important not to see
these things and see how much it moved and think to yourself 'if I would have got a piece of that.' That's
not that kind of move. That's not what this is; this is a trap. This is a very difficult thing to convey to new
traders or new students because they think somebody absolutely bought the low down here or in this area
and then sold it up here. They believe that, and I'm telling you that didn't happen. If it happened, they'll
gladly go into their broker account and log in live, log into it with their password blocked out, that's fine, but
go in there and show their time and date and their time and sales fills on a reputable broker. You're not
going to see that, okay? Believe me, folks, if I could do that in this report, I would be doing it every month,
every time. I would do that just to show you, you know, this is the way it is. But I'm telling you up front
because I want to protect you, that this type of market driver is essentially risking ruin.

- LIQUIDITY RUNS
But we can identify and use it, like I teach to use the non-farm payroll. Non-farm payroll, I don't trade that
report. You can trade after the non-farm payroll report, you can trade after the CPI number, but ahead of it?
No, because you have no idea. You have no idea. I have no idea where it's going to go on the CPI number.
So what makes you think you do as a green horn, someone who just started a couple months watching
some videos from me, some yahoo that teaches with a demo account? You think you're going to be able to
beat this? No. Don't do that. You're going to create scar tissue.
So what I did this morning, I outlined how to focus on what to look for and what you would study. In a
minute or less, I drew out, 'here's where the buy side is.' Why did I call this out? Look at the idea of it not
wanting to go higher. So retail sees this as what? Strong resistance. So they can be short in their mind, and
the stop losses would be above these highs here in the form of buy stops. That's what we call buy side
liquidity. I took you over here to the relative equal lows. What's going to be resting below that? Sell stops,
which is why we call that sell side liquidity. Both sides of the marketplace are always in contention for high
impact news drivers like CPI, rate announcements, non-farm payroll, those types of things. Anytime you
see in your lower time frame charts these smooth edges like this, very, very smooth relative equal lows,
very, very smooth relative equal highs, they are going to be made jagged, meaning it's going to rip through
it like it does over here. So now they're not so smooth already.

Now, if this whole movement down here was a function to offset any positions or interest because it
controls sentiment as well, with orders resting below here, up here rather, down here, looking at the relative
equal highs, the market ran for those as well. And then what has it done since? It's staying in between
those two pools of liquidity. But what did it do first? It went right to the Fair Value Gap I mentioned, overshot
it by a little bit, which is understandable given the nature of this report that came out. And then inside of all
this move, it dropped down. Now, you can't see it like this, so if I'm in a one-minute chart, here's the 8:30
candle. It opens, trades up, it looks like it completely rolls right on through it. But we saw momentarily,
before it ran up higher, it ran up to it here and stopped right in here. It stopped and then moved lower, then
came all the way back up and overlapped the entire run and then went to the buy side. If I show you on a
30-second chart here, it opens, rallies up, drops down. Still 15-second chart, there's the move right there,
that's what it looked like. It opened, ran up, this came to this big green candle here, didn't exist yet. Watch
the video. You can watch it where it opens, it trades up, creates that short-term high there, then it runs for
the sell side, and then all in the same one-minute candle, it opens here on this 15-second candle and then
runs for the buy side. Then now we're hanging around in here.

section_title: POST-CPI ES FORECAST AND THE ALGORITHM'S UNDERLYING CONTROL
content:
So I think we could make a potential run higher to take out this high. This run down here looks a little
aggressive. It feels like they went down there fastest, and then this movement here, this is a lot of that run
- break-retest-go-short. I think traders are trying to go short from the retail perspective, and they would want
to reach up into this Wick here. I would observe and study with that idea in mind. That would look like this.
It's not a trade, folks, it's just this is what you're doing when you're reading the tape. It gives you experience
watching price action where you don't push the button, even in a demo. You get a feel for what price is likely
to do. You spend a lot of time doing that.
If I would have done that in the beginning, I would have spared myself years of drawing down, blowing
accounts, and it would have been a lot faster for me to find consistency. So here's the level I'm referring to:
4407 and then 4405 even, that's the midpoint of this Gap here. Okay, so watch and see if they want to draw
up into that. The main takeaway what I was saying was even in this fast market move, the Fair Value Gap I
mentioned and the liquidity pools, it went to the Fair Value Gap, then dropped to the sell side, then ran for
the buy side. So the takeaway would be, even in that speed, okay, of within one minute, the very levels that
I gave you, the algorithm referred to immediately in a way, in a speed and a velocity that even I can't
participate in it. So the takeaway is for you to see that even in this recklessness that looks like chaos and
just carnage, it's still being controlled, but it's being controlled in a way where it's going to move a lot in a
short span of time, but you're not being permitted to profit during it. That's what I'm trying to convey to you,
it's an illusion. These types of moves are the very things that draw people into wanting to trade because
they think they can get in here and take these trades and make your 20, 30 handles in a minute,
overleveraged, and get a five-figure withdrawal. So don't fall for that trap. It's very, very important you don't
fall for that trap.

They run into our consequent encroachment I mentioned they would draw to. That's enough to pay your
bills right there, folks. I know it doesn't seem like it's all that sexy, but that little move right there, that's what
a high frequency trading algorithm does. It trades on that type of little move hundreds of times per session.
Now look at the reaction of that. Just that little move right there, that in itself is a high frequency trading
algorithm entry. It moves back and forth. There's algorithms that trade one-sided, they stay in a buy model
for buys or going long, and there's other high frequency trading algorithms that trade on the sell side, it's a
sell side model so it's only going short when it's offering the opportunity to do so. And then you have a
model, a high frequency trading algorithm that simply goes all over the place. It takes whatever volatility is
available at the time, and it runs up, down, up, down, up, down, up, down. That's the stuff, because I get
asked all the time 'what is it that you won't teach us?' It's that. I'm not allowed to teach you that, I'm not
going to teach that, but I have demonstrated it before in the past. And yeah, I'm very candid about saying
I'm not going to teach that part to you.

section_title: THE POWER OF FOCUSING ON ONE TRADING MODEL
content:
But instead of saying look at how much you've already taught and look how much the impact has been for
other people, they're making real money with it now. The point of appreciation is lost because you forget
everything I've already shared openly and it works amazingly well. And because I say I can't do something,
like I can't, folks, I can't, literally I cannot, I'm not permitted to do it. I would love to do it, trust me. I get real
close, as close as I can. And the students that really, really, really want to know it, they think that I've hidden
it in my teachings. I did not. Okay, I did not teach anything like that. I'm not allowed to do it. But you don't
need that. All you need to do is find one little multiplier, one little thing that gives you a setup, one little thing
that gives you a reason to engage the marketplace. Measure risk, define it that means know where your
stop should be and then where you should take profits (partial) and where you think term, where's your
best case exit.
Sometimes my trades don't go to full terminus because I'm illustrating either a point of not needing to be
100 accurate, so that way it encourages you as my students. Or sometimes as a human, I just don't do it
correctly. Or I'll take off more than I should have. In hindsight, I'll look back and say, 'I wish I left a little bit
more on the trade.' That part is always going to be there no matter how good you get at this. You're always
going to be wrong. Even in your winning trades, you're going to wish you would have had more, held on
longer, got out sooner. Yeah, it's always like that. So this craft that you're involved in now is a lifestyle. It's
not, you learn how to make money and that's it, you've reached your destination. Excellence is a pursuit; it's
not a destination, it's final. It's always, always going to be something that you're striving to do better and
reach for. And mine is my exits, and I've been open about that as well. I always want to make them better
because I have lots of ways to get into things, but I'm never fully satisfied if I'm going to be honest with you.
I'm never satisfied ever with my exits. And I have got out on the highest tick a handful of times, and I got in
and out of trades on the lowest tick a handful of times. But you would think with 30 years, I would have a
whole lot more examples. I don't, because I'm either trying to outthink my algorithm or I'm trying to find
something that I haven't identified yet. And because of those distractions, my mind's going a thousand
miles an hour, it's hard for me to focus, which is another reason why I don't like to have people chatting to
me. And even when my son's next to me, they're not allowed to say anything to me because that small little
distraction, it takes me off my train of thought. And you have to, especially for someone like me, I have to
be very dialed in, and I'm very easily distracted. I'm like a dog that sees a squirrel. I can't focus. So maybe
you don't have that issue in your trading. Maybe you can multitask, you know, and do exceedingly well even
as a trader. I don't have that skill set. I can't. I have to be 100% dialed in because I have so many things
that I can see as an opportunity. See, where is you, you have one model or two, two things, maybe three
things that you learn from me. And if one of those things occur, it's easy for you to focus, okay, I see this.
But you have to understand, like I have 80 plus things that are jumping off the chart at me. Which one do I
want to eat off of? I'm like a dog in a meat market turned loose. Do I want to run over there and bite that?
Do I want to run over here and bite that? That's the closest analogy I can give to you. I see so many things
I can see as an opportunity in price, whether it be a three handle run, a 10 handle run, a 15 handle run, a
run that goes eight handles, reverses for four, runs for 13. Those those things are jumping off the chart all
the time to me. And depending upon what my mind is focused on at the time, or if I'm if I have a means of
sitting down trying to explain something to an audience, or if I'm doing a lecture or whatever, or if I'm
teaching my son, whatever one I go with, that's the one I go with. And I'm always, this is the part that you
mean, I'm saying I'm always regretful that I didn't do one or the other because I have so many of them to
choose from. You think that if I sat down and I divulged all of them to you that it's going to make you a
better trader? It doesn't. It's almost like, well, if you have more than one child, which one's your your
favorite? You can't say which one's your favorite, right? But when you're in the market and you're trading
and you're looking at price action, it is very difficult for me to say, I love this one more than that one. So
whenever I come to the decision on this is the one I'm going to go with, that's it. But the whole time price is
running, I know my mind at certain times that the other one's going to do certain things, and then price

because I've been honest with you and told you I'm not going to teach you everything, and it's not

And the most important thing is where price is going to go. Because literally, there's so many ways to get

lower time frames are wonderful for entry and in managing risk. But if your trade is entirely linked to and

You need movement, folks. As a trader, we need displacement and a change from one price to another

in a trade longer than 10 minutes. And 10 minutes is long-term position trading to you. And I'm not saying
that that's a bad thing, because if you decide on what your main comfort zone is, how long can you hold a

then you will do very, very well in intraday charts working with one-minute time frames, and you can get

section_title: CONTENTMENT, DISTRIBUTION AND THE ALGORITHM'S AUTHOR
content:
Are you trading to be the best social media trader? Are you training to be the guy that makes the biggest
pip haul or point haul, the guy that tries to get the highest high and the lowest low? What is it that you're
